Criteria: Heat index values are expected to reach or exceed 105°F (or higher depending on regional climatological thresholds) for at least 2 days, and nighttime lows do not drop below 75°F. Poses a severe threat to life.
Conditions are favorable for an excessive heat event to meet or exceed warning criteria within the next 24 to 72 hours. Permits advance planning.
Heat index values are expected to reach 100°F to 104°F for at least 2 hours. Dehydration and heat exhaustion are likely with prolonged exposure.
Local governance declarations triggering the activation of community cooling centers, emergency energy grid protocols, and mandatory structural checks for vulnerable demographics.
